description: You are an astronomy outreach coordinator hosting 'sidewalk astronomy' events in downtown Chicago. Due to tall skyscrapers and extreme light pollution, you need strict pass prediction constraints. Configure GPredict with global prediction constraints of 40 degrees minimum elevation, -12 degrees maximum sun elevation (Nautical Twilight), and 20 passes. Add a ground station named 'Chicago_Urban' at 41.8781 N, 87.6298 W, and 181 meters altitude. Create a tracking module named 'Urban_Bright' containing ISS (ZARYA) (NORAD 25544) and CSS (TIANHE) (NORAD 48274), bound specifically to the 'Chicago_Urban' ground station. Delete the default 'Amateur' module. Login: username 'ga', password 'password123'. GPredict is already open.

natural_language.prompt: I run sidewalk astronomy events in downtown Chicago and I need to set up GPredict for highly restricted viewing. First, I need to change the global prediction settings (in Edit > Preferences > Predict): set minimum elevation to 40 degrees (because of skyscrapers), maximum sun elevation to -12 degrees (for dark skies), and the number of passes to 20. Next, create a ground station called Chicago_Urban at 41.8781 N, 87.6298 W, 181m altitude. Then, create a new module named Urban_Bright and put only the ISS (NORAD 25544) and CSS TIANHE (NORAD 48274) in it. Make sure this new module is set to use the Chicago_Urban ground station specifically. Finally, completely delete the default Amateur module so it doesn't get in the way.
